Metadata.Case_Name: AMG GLOBAL INVESTMENTS & HOLDINGS PTE LTD (IN LIQUIDATION) v ONG KEE MING RICHARD & Anor
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd (in Liquidation)

Facts.Text: AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore.
AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd had objects of trading in metals and providing financial services.
From October 2007 onwards, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd had three directors, namely Mr Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan, Mr Ong Kee Ming Richard, and Mr Koh Ting Giap.

During the period 23 August 2017 to 21 September 2017, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd maintained a bank account with Standard Chartered Bank Singapore Limited that required joint signatures of Mr Manu and Mr Ong.

On 25 August 2017, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd received US$2,764,104.53 into its Standard Chartered Bank account pursuant to a documentary letter of credit issued by Kasikornbank Public Company Limited under a monetising arrangement with Food & Beverage Cap Co Ltd.

Under a memorandum of understanding dated 21 June 2017, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd agreed to pay 75% of the documentary letter of credit value to Food & Beverage Cap Co Ltd and 5% to White Lotus Cosmetics Limited, retaining 20% as a non-recourse payment.
By 23 January 2018, Mr Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan had become the 100% shareholder of AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd.
For many years prior to 2017, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd carried out very little business but incurred expenses including rental of premises.
On 9 March 2018, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd was wound up following an unsatisfied statutory demand dated 22 November 2017 for US$2,145,000 issued by Food & Beverage Cap Co Ltd.
Upon winding up, a liquidator from AAG Corporate Advisory Pte Ltd was appointed for AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd.

After the inflow of funds in August 2017, AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd made a series of payments and cash withdrawals from its Standard Chartered Bank account between 25 August 2017 and 2 July 2018.

AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd, acting by its liquidator, commenced Suit No 881 of 2020 against its former directors Mr Ong Kee Ming Richard and Mr Koh Ting Giap in relation to withdrawals and payments from its bank account and the use of a banking facility.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the former directors of AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd breached their duties and incurred liability to the company in relation to withdrawals, payments, and use of a banking facility involving the company’s funds.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors of a company must act bona fide in the interests of the company, for proper corporate purposes, avoid conflicts between personal interests and duties, and exercise reasonable skill and care, and may be personally liable for unauthorised or improper use of company assets.
Party_Details.Application: The court examined the inflow of funds into AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd’s bank account, the subsequent pattern of withdrawals and payments authorised or allowed by the former directors, the absence or presence of commercial justification for those transactions, and the directors’ knowledge, state of mind, and reliance on explanations given by a fellow director in order to determine whether their conduct complied with their duties to the company.
Party_Details.Conclusion: Judgment entered for AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd (in Liquidation) against its former directors for specified sums with interest.

Metadata.Case_Name: AMG GLOBAL INVESTMENTS & HOLDINGS PTE LTD (IN LIQUIDATION) v ONG KEE MING RICHARD & Anor
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Ong Kee Ming Richard

Facts.Text: The first defendant, Mr Ong Kee Ming Richard, is a Singapore citizen.
Mr Ong was a director of AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd from 13 September 2006 to 25 September 2017.
From October 2007 onwards, Mr Ong served as a director of AMG together with Mr Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan and Mr Koh Ting Giap.
Mr Ong was a joint signatory with Mr Manu to AMG's bank account with Standard Chartered Bank (Singapore) Limited between 23 August 2017 and 21 September 2017.
Mr Ong countersigned nineteen withdrawals or payments from AMG's Standard Chartered Bank account between 25 August 2017 and 21 September 2017.

On or about 11 August 2017, Mr Ong obtained a handwritten confirmation from Mr Manu stating that Mr Manu took full responsibility for payments from AMG's bank account and for the procurement and supply of goods mentioned in the documentary letter of credit.
Mr Ong signed a security agreement with Standard Chartered Bank for a facility secured against a fixed deposit of US$1,000,000 in AMG's name.
Mr Ong authorised a payment of US$74,699.11 from AMG's bank account to himself which he regarded as remuneration for his services as a director.
Mr Ong authorised a payment of US$149,398.22 from AMG's bank account to Mr Abdul Razeed after expressing concerns about the size of the payment.

Mr Ong authorised payments from AMG's bank account to various third parties including Sabre Global Pte Ltd, Three Quality Services Limited, Kevin Anthony Griffin Jr, Quality Printers Pte Ltd and others during late August and September 2017.
In September 2017 Mr Ong attended Clementi Polyclinic and was referred to the National University Hospital for investigation of a possible psychosis episode.
Mr Ong was warded at the Institute of Mental Health for about three weeks from 15 October 2017 after he had resigned as a director of AMG.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Mr Ong, as a director and joint bank signatory of AMG, breached his fiduciary and statutory duties and incurred liability, including for knowing receipt, in authorising or receiving various payments and withdrawals from AMG's bank account between 25 August 2017 and 21 September 2017.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors owe fiduciary duties to act bona fide in the interests of the company, for proper corporate purposes, to avoid conflicts of interest, and to exercise reasonable skill and care, as reflected in the general law and section 157 of the Companies Act; a director who authorises company payments must honestly and reasonably believe they are for proper corporate purposes and must take reasonable steps to understand and verify the transactions; a person is liable in knowing receipt where company assets are disposed of in breach of fiduciary duty, the person beneficially receives those assets, and knows of the breach such that it is unconscionable to retain the benefit.
Party_Details.Application: The court examined Mr Ong's role as director and joint signatory in relation to each payment and withdrawal from AMG's bank account, considering what information he had, what explanations he received from Mr Manu, whether he obtained supporting documentation, and when he began to suspect that funds were being removed for purposes not connected to AMG's business. The court assessed whether Mr Ong could reasonably rely on Mr Manu's representations in the context of a small company without internal control systems, and evaluated specific transactions such as cash withdrawals, payments to third parties, and a payment to himself as director's remuneration to determine if they were for proper corporate purposes or involved unauthorised self-benefit. The court also considered Mr Ong's mental health evidence, his obtaining of a written confirmation from Mr Manu assuming responsibility, and his expressed reluctance and objections to certain large payments, in deciding whether his conduct met the standards of honesty and reasonableness required of a director and whether any receipt of company funds by him was made with knowledge that it was traceable to a breach of fiduciary duty.
Party_Details.Conclusion: Judgment was entered against Mr Ong, with the court allowing AMG's claims against him in the sum of US$1,297,883.63 together with interest.

Metadata.Case_Name: AMG GLOBAL INVESTMENTS & HOLDINGS PTE LTD (IN LIQUIDATION) v ONG KEE MING RICHARD & Anor
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Koh Ting Giap

Facts.Text: Koh Ting Giap is a Singapore citizen.
Koh Ting Giap was a director of AMG Global Investments & Holdings Pte Ltd from 13 September 2006 to 10 January 2014 and again from 25 September 2017 until the company was wound up.
Koh Ting Giap was the registered owner of 1,080,000 shares in AMG, of which 1,079,996 shares were held as nominee on trust for Weeds Natural Remedies Pte Ltd.
Through Weeds Natural Remedies Pte Ltd, Koh Ting Giap arranged for AMG to receive formulae for natural health supplements and an eight-year licence to use certain registered trade marks.
Before leaving AMG around early 2014, Koh Ting Giap worked together with co-directors Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan and Ong Kee Ming Richard.
Koh Ting Giap left AMG around 2014 because AMG had done very little business and its anticipated deals had not materialised.
When he rejoined AMG in 2017, Koh Ting Giap agreed to serve as the locally resident director at the request of Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan.
Upon rejoining AMG in 2017, Koh Ting Giap agreed that Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan would be the 100% shareholder and sole bank account signatory of AMG.
On 26 September 2017, Koh Ting Giap signed a directors' resolution authorising Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan to be the sole signatory for AMG's banking facilities with Standard Chartered Bank.
After rejoining AMG in 2017, Koh Ting Giap discussed with Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan that AMG should repay him part of the money he had previously advanced to the company.
On 21 September 2017, AMG paid US$37,458.77 to Koh Ting Giap from its Standard Chartered Bank account.
On 29 September 2017, AMG paid US$37,247.43 to Koh Ting Giap from its Standard Chartered Bank account.
Between late September 2017 and October 2017, AMG made twelve cash withdrawals or cash cheques totalling US$148,064.97 from its Standard Chartered Bank account while Koh Ting Giap was a director.
On 2 October 2017, AMG paid US$10,000 from its Standard Chartered Bank account to Uniroyal Communications Pte Ltd while Koh Ting Giap was a director.
On 2 October 2017, AMG paid US$11,000 from its Standard Chartered Bank account to Ms Chua Pair Shen while Koh Ting Giap was a director.
On 3 October 2017, AMG paid US$11,500 from its Standard Chartered Bank account to Mr Shuan Jarmen while Koh Ting Giap was a director.
On 11 October 2017, AMG paid US$3,700 from its Standard Chartered Bank account to Ms Christina Marie Nyanmani while Koh Ting Giap was a director.
While Koh Ting Giap was a director, AMG made a payment of US$1,370 from its Standard Chartered Bank account to Mr Ng Wai Loon.
While Koh Ting Giap was a director, AMG made a payment of US$1,850 from its Standard Chartered Bank account to Mr Abdul Razeed.
On or about 9 November 2017, after a drawdown on a Standard Chartered Bank facility secured on AMG's fixed deposit, AMG paid US$744,000 to Smart Recycling Pte Ltd.
When he rejoined AMG in 2017, Koh Ting Giap understood that his role as director included responsibility for the company's accounts.
In 2009, Koh Ting Giap emailed his co-directors stating that he had contributed or advanced several hundred thousand Singapore dollars to AMG in cash, loans, office expenses, and office renovation.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Koh Ting Giap, as a director and recipient of company funds, breached his directors' duties and incurred liability in respect of withdrawals and payments made from AMG's bank account, including sums paid to himself before and after rejoining the board and sums paid out under a bank facility.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors must act bona fide in the interests of the company, for proper corporate purposes, avoid conflicts between personal interests and duties, and exercise reasonable skill and care, including taking reasonable steps to understand and monitor company payments; a director who allows company assets to be misapplied or who benefits from such misapplication may be liable for breach of fiduciary duty or for knowing receipt where he receives trust property with knowledge making it unconscionable to retain the benefit.
Party_Details.Application: The court examined Koh Ting Giap's decision to rejoin AMG as director while seeking repayment of his past advances, his agreement that Manu Poovannunilkunnathil Kuttappan would be sole bank signatory, his knowledge that the previous director had resisted signing certain cheques, his lack of safeguards over AMG's bank account, and his receipt of payments from AMG and the bank facility drawdown, to assess whether he acted honestly and reasonably in protecting AMG's assets and whether his personal interest in recovering loans conflicted with his duties.
Party_Details.Conclusion: Judgment entered against Koh Ting Giap, with AMG's claims allowed in the sum of US$1,017,070.93 plus interest.
